response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack and web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but the role is fundamentally a C++ systems engineering position. His C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE, plus a bachelor's-thesis audio project — far below the depth required for architecting high-performance, multi-threaded, STL-heavy industrial applications. He has no industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 cybersecurity background, which are core to this role. The web-stack overlap (Node.js, React) listed as advantageous skills does not compensate for the absence of the primary technical requirement.
strengths: Proven event-driven and distributed-systems architecture experience at scale (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second; GISCollective: Kubernetes-deployed geo-data platform)
Solid agile engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a track record of mentoring through workshops and talks
Exposure to Node.js and React — both listed as advantageous skills in the job description
concerns: C++ experience is superficial — limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a student thesis project, with no evidence of STL, multi-threading, or performance-critical systems work
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems background; no familiarity with MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or IEC 61850/104
No secure development lifecycle (SDL) or IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience, which is an explicit requirement of the role
